body_txt: When Jack insists that Tom apologize to Harry. This trope comes in two forms, based on where he gets the ability to insist: In both cases, expect a deeply grudging apology that will manage to keep things going for at least a little while — with Teeth-Clenched Teamwork . Jack may put his foot down on a Backhanded Apology . Then again, he may not. The Unapologetic character will usually defy this trope, showing that s/he isn't sorry for his/her actions. Though rare, a Rejected Apology can come from this due to the implicit insincerity of the apology, which may even result in Harry getting angry at Jack for trying this. You may be forced into this if you're Made Out to Be a Jerkass . This can easily overlap with Obeying Under Protest , when the apology is clearly delivered without an apologetic spirit.
